danger_signals.visual_form: Red spheres displayed above defeated enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Direct feedback of enemy remaining vitality; more spheres = greater threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Celestial object traversing sky during story progression
danger_signals.meaning: Impending world catastrophe and primary antagonistic force

danger_signals.visual_form: Comet transforms into Dark Gaia entity at climax
danger_signals.meaning: Abstract evil made tangible; final boss form of world threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental storytelling through ruined expedition sites
danger_signals.meaning: Previous explorers failed to survive; foreshadows environmental hazards

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated safe zones with Gaia entity present
safety_signals.meaning: Save points and healing locations; narratively linked to world salvation

safety_signals.visual_form: Gems dropped after clearing enemy rooms
safety_signals.meaning: Progression reward; permanent stat increases indicate safety checkpoint

safety_signals.visual_form: Gold orbs dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Health point recovery resource; threshold at 100+ points allows stage restart safety

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Transformation ability; associated with exploration and area access
Combat-focused form with enhanced damage and reach; locked areas require form
Ultimate power form; represents union of Light and Dark Knights
Present in all Dark Spaces; statues of alter egos flank entity; represents world salvation directive

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Comet transformed into ancient Blazer War weapon
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Approaches throughout game; causes world disruption and evolution interference

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Empty-handed or missing explorers; ruined sites with no survivors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Environment shows signs of abandonment; hazards designed to kill intruders

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Celestial object visible in sky; grows more prominent as game progresses
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: World NPCs aware of impending doom; story dialogue references approaching catastrophe

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jewel rewards appear; room becomes visually 'safe' with no spawn indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies do not respawn unless player exits and re-enters area; permanent stat gains indicate cleared state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sacred space with Gaia entity and statue trio (Will, Freedan, Shadow)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Health recovery enabled; form transformation possible; save point function; ability gain opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Culmination location where Will revealed as Dark Knight, Kara as Light Knight
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Firebird ultimate power unleashed; transformation to Shadow final form; narrative memory-wipe begins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Modern-day school setting; ambiguous 'real world' aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters retain friendship despite memory loss; cyclical narrative suggesting illusion breaking

environmental_storytelling.example: Great Pyramid, Great Wall of China, Angkor Wat, Incan ruins, Nazca Lines serve as level environments and narrative anchors
Game setting mirrors 16th century exploration era with Christopher Columbus references; creates historical grounding for fantasy narrative
Environmental design suggests previous explorers died in these locations; hazard placement and empty areas imply danger history
Mystic Statues scattered across world ruins; collection represents piecing together world salvation puzzle

environmental_storytelling.technique: Dark Space as safe haven design
environmental_storytelling.example: Contrast between dangerous ruins and sacred Dark Space zones communicates sanctuary through visual sanctuary design

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Flute-based techniques enable area access; incidental combat capability; primary interaction method
Sword attacks deal more damage with longer reach; access to combat-restricted areas; higher strength stat
Highest damage output; pseudopod weapon; final form manifestation; requires Dark Knight + Light Knight union
Do not respawn unless area exited and re-entered; defeated enemies grant no bonuses on re-encounter; creates permanent area progression
Stationary in Dark Spaces; grants abilities and form transformations; narrative authority on world salvation; flanked by statue representations
Approaches throughout game narrative; revealed as ancient Blazer War weapon; manifests as boss entity; cannot be avoided or negotiated with

notes: Illusion of Gaia uses minimalist visual communication—emphasis on form transformation (Will/Freedan/Shadow) as core identity system rather than color-coded faction markers. Dark Spaces serve dual function as both narrative safe havens and mechanical save points, embedding story safety into game structure. World threat (comet/Dark Gaia) is abstract and approaching rather than territorial or political. No traditional faction conflict; instead, opposition between world spirit (Gaia) salvation directive versus destructive ancient weapon (Dark Gaia). Narrative ambiguity in ending (memory erasure, modern-day school setting) suggests layer between 'real' world and explored fantasy world. Exploration paradigm privileges puzzle-like area access gating over faction-based barriers.
